core_payload.content:
# Personal AI Assistant Governance Control

## 1. Role
Personal AI Assistant Governance Control owns the visibility and decision surfaces that keep assistant action trustworthy. It binds provider posture, runtime visibility, review lanes, branch posture, notification, and integration access into one bounded control domain so the assistant remains inspectable and governable at the point of use.

## 2. Governance Control Surface
This sub-hub groups `ai-wallet`, `hybrid-llm-access`, `ai-control-surface`, `agent-control-plane`, `branch-steward-agent`, `review-agent`, `notification`, `api-gateway`, and `integrations`. Together they define provider readiness, control visibility, policy-bearing runtime state, branch and review discipline, escalation signals, and external access posture.

## 3. Operating Boundary
This sub-hub does not own conversational ingress, continuity shaping, or delegated execution work itself. Those remain in `conversation-ingress`, `memory-continuity`, and `execution-orchestration` so governance stays interpretive and visible rather than silently fused into every runtime step.

neuro_concentrate.summary: Personal AI Assistant Governance Control groups the provider, control-plane, review, branch, notification, and integration surfaces that keep the assistant governable. It binds AI Wallet, Hybrid LLM Access, AI Control Surface, Agent Control Plane, Branch Steward, Review Agent, Notification, API Gateway, and Integrations into one visible control domain. This boundary matters because users need clear visibility into cost, access, review posture, and runtime authority before they trust the assistant to route meaningful work.

epistemic_ledger.rationale: Minted during Personal AI Assistant decomposition to isolate wallet, review, branch, notification, and integration governance from conversational and execution surfaces.
